concerns.text: FR-001 (≥1000 human samples) and FR-002 (≥50 LLM samples) are explicitly rejected by the plan in favor of a 150/150 design. However, tasks T012 and T013 implement the 150/150 design without a corresponding task to formally document or track the deviation from the spec's hard constraints. The implementer has no task to update the spec or record the 'methodology-f30244be' rejection in the project state, leaving the spec/tasks contradiction unresolved in the implementation artifacts.

FR-007 requires 'stratified subsampling or bootstrapping' to handle sample size imbalance. The plan explicitly REJECTS FR-007 in favor of a 'Balanced Blocked Design'. There is no task in tasks.md to implement the rejection logic, document the rationale, or update the spec to reflect that FR-007 is no longer applicable. The spec requirement exists but has no matching task (neither implementation nor formal rejection).

US-3 Acceptance Scenario 1 mandates a Shapiro-Wilk test followed by Mann-Whitney U or Welch's t-test. The plan and tasks (T026) implement a 'Blocked Permutation Test' instead. While the plan justifies this as a methodological improvement, there is no task to explicitly handle the rejection of the spec's mandated statistical test or to document the switch in the code comments/report. The spec requirement is unimplemented and unaddressed in the task list.

Tasks T012 and T013 reference 'Issue/PR descriptions' for task derivation but do not explicitly tag the specific User Story (US-1) in the task description line (e.g., '[US1]') as consistently as other tasks (e.g., T014, T015). While the section header implies US-1, the lack of explicit [US1] tags on T012/T013 reduces traceability clarity for the coverage check.
